HomeWalksHistoryBathEat & DrinkStay

Local Food Guide

Eat and Drink

Walking times are from 14c St Margaret's Street. All places listed have decent options for dietary restrictions.

All walkable from the cottage

Quick timings included

Mix of coffee, brunch, pubs, and dinner

Photo coming soon

Pipit

2-3 mins

Best pastries and coffee in town.

Photo: Charles Miller (CC BY 2.0)

Little Rituals

2-3 mins

Great coffee and atmosphere, a local favourite.

Photo: Little Rituals website

Thai Barn

1-2 mins

Family favourite Thai.

Photo: Thai Barn website

The Bridge Tea Rooms

4 mins

Highly regarded traditional and cosy spot for afternoon tea.

Photo: Rob Farrow (CC BY-SA 2.0)

Photo coming soon

FIG

3 mins

New brunch spot in the Shambles.

Photo: Diliff (CC BY-SA 3.0)

Photo coming soon

Ravello

3 mins

Classic Italian.

Photo: Local BOA photo

Three Horseshoes

1 min

Local pub; food trucks and live music.

Photo: Jaggery (CC BY-SA 2.0)

Castle Inn

8-10 mins uphill

Good food, views, and cider.

Photo: The Castle Inn website

Photo coming soon

The Little Barn

15-18 mins

Family-friendly cafe with exceptional food next to Tithe Barn.

Photo: Local BOA photo

The George

22-25 mins

Exceptional cooking and off the beaten track.

Photo: Geograph / Wikimedia Commons

Photo coming soon

The Bunch of Grapes

45-55 mins

Characterful countryside pub-restaurant known for quality food.

Photo: Local BOA photo

Photo coming soon

The DogHouse

3 mins

Pet friendly cafe (outdoor seating only) and pet shop.

Photo: Local BOA photo
